rationale: In the price-promotion literature review, the citation is tied to a specific mechanism (purchase reinforcement) rather than being a generic list item. It is used to motivate one side of the paper's conceptual argument that discounting can have positive long-term effects on base sales. The citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's decomposition model or directly build on its method, so the role is conceptual framing support.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ailawadi et al. (2007) as prior empirical support that promotion-induced stockpiling/purchase reinforcement can create positive longer-term sales effects. This helps frame the citing paper's setup that the long-term impact of discounting is theoretically mixed and must be tested empirically.
